methodsAttempted.query: site:education.lu.se Lund University AI ChatGPT examination policy students
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official Lund University education pages for GenAI teacher guidance, Q&A, and the English policy PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: site:staff.lu.se generative AI Lund University guidance students examination ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official staff AI page with policy summary, Copilot, DeepL, and ChatGPT Edu guidance.
Swedish-language discovery exposed the Swedish policy PDF and older report/category pages; the Swedish policy PDF was selected as the canonical Swedish policy source.

methodsAttempted.query: site:lunduniversity.lu.se Lund University generative AI students policy OR guidelines
methodsAttempted.notes: Public web search surfaced Lund publication records and AI expert/news pages; publication/research records were not used as policy evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:campusonline.lu.se Lund University generative AI students privacy Copilot Gemini
methodsAttempted.notes: Official student-facing Campus Online guidance was followed from the teacher guidance page and fetched directly.

artifacts.summary: Official Lund University sources were found on medarbetarwebben.lu.se, education.lu.se, staff.lu.se, and campusonline.lu.se. Firecrawl search and fetch were sufficient; Playwright was not needed. A Lund publication record was rejected because it is a research/publication page, not current policy or operational guidance.

stopConditions: Stop after verified university-wide or general Lund University AI policy/guidance sources have been staged.
Do not use publication records, event pages, research showcases, or faculty-only pages for university-wide claims.
Do not bypass login-required support-center pages linked from official staff guidance.

artifacts.claimText: Lund University's Swedish generative AI policy encourages staff and students to explore and use generative AI responsibly and creatively within the stated policy principles.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Questions and answers on GenAI tools
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At Lund University, it is permissible to use generative AI tools in education if you believe they can contribute to or facilitate learning. However, there are some important things to consider. As a teacher, you need to: be able to confirm that students have achieved the program's and course's learning objectives; be aware that sharing students' work with GAI tools is not allowed; ensure that every student has equal access to the GAI tools you recommend; inform students about the risks of sharing personal information or copyrighted material with the tools; inform students about the rules for using GAI tools in the course or program.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Questions about rules and guidelines - What are the guidelines at Lund University?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Questions and answers on GenAI tools

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for teachers in five steps
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Once you have reviewed learning objectives and made decisions about the use of GenAI products in teaching, you must inform your students. Be clear about whether and, if so, how students can use GenAI products in their work. It is also important to inform students about the consequences of unauthorized use of GenAI products:

artifacts.sourceTitle: How to use generative AI responsibly
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If you wish to use GenAI for a compulsory assignment or in an examination, you must always double-check with the teacher whether it is permitted and, if so, how you should report its use. Even if it is not clearly stated in the course materials that GenAI is prohibited, it may still be considered cheating if you make it appear as though you have created something yourself that a GenAI tool has generated for you.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: How to use generative AI responsibly
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: When studying, you should primarily use the tools for which Lund University holds licences. As a student at Lund University, you can use Microsoft Copilot Chat and Google Gemini by logging in with your student account. However, even if you are logged in with your student account, you must never upload, for example, other students' work, sensitive personal data or copyright-protected material - this applies to text as well as images, audio or video.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Protect your privacy and that of others

artifacts.title: Lund University staged AI policy crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged machine-reviewed candidates for Lund University based on official university sources only. The run found a Swedish university policy PDF plus official education, staff, and student guidance covering responsible use, learning support, student/teacher communication, privacy, approved or licensed tools, and ChatGPT Edu access. Publication/research records were rejected as non-policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Coverage is intentionally limited to official, fetchable Lund University sources found in this run; faculty-, department-, course-, and login-only support-center rules were not crawled.
The staged claims are machine-reviewed candidates only and are not published as canonical public policy claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
